0.text: I, Jarom, write a few words as my father Enos commanded me, so our genealogy may be preserved.

Since these plates are small, and since these things are written for the benefit of our brothers the Lamanites, I must keep it brief. I will not write about my own prophesying or revelations. What more could I write than my fathers have already written? Have they not revealed the plan of salvation? I say yes, and that is enough for me.

Much work needed to be done among this people because of the hardness of their hearts, the deafness of their ears, the blindness of their minds, and the stubbornness of their necks. Nevertheless, God is extremely merciful to them and has not yet swept them off the face of the land.

Many among us have many revelations, because not all are stubborn. Those who are not stubborn and who have faith receive communion with the Holy Spirit, which reveals things to the children of men according to their faith.

Two hundred years had passed, and the people of Nephi had grown strong in the land. They kept the law of Moses and kept the sabbath day holy to the Lord. They did not swear or blaspheme. The laws of the land were extremely strict.
They were spread across much of the land, and so were the Lamanites. The Lamanites were far more numerous than the Nephites. They loved murder and would drink the blood of animals.

They came against us, the Nephites, many times to battle. But our kings and leaders were mighty men of faith in the Lord. They taught the people the ways of the Lord. Therefore we withstood the Lamanites and drove them out of our lands. We began to fortify our cities and all our places of inheritance.

We multiplied greatly and spread across the land. We became extremely rich in gold, silver, precious things, fine woodwork, buildings, machinery, and also iron, copper, brass, and steel. We made all kinds of tools to farm the ground, and weapons of war — the sharp-pointed arrow, the quiver, the dart, the javelin, and all preparations for war.

Being prepared to face the Lamanites, they did not prevail against us. The word of the Lord was confirmed — what he had told our fathers: "As long as you keep my commandments, you will prosper in the land."
The prophets of the Lord warned the people of Nephi that if they did not keep the commandments but fell into sin, they would be destroyed from the face of the land.

The prophets, priests, and teachers worked diligently, urging the people with great patience to be diligent. They taught the law of Moses and the purpose behind it, persuading them to look forward to the Messiah and believe in him as if he had already come. This is how they taught them.
By doing this, they kept the people from being destroyed. They pricked their hearts with the word, continually stirring them up to repentance.
Two hundred and thirty-eight years had passed — years filled with wars, conflicts, and disagreements for much of the time.

I, Jarom, will not write more because the plates are small. But, my brothers and sisters, you can go to the other plates of Nephi, because on them the records of our wars are engraved, according to the writings of the kings.
I deliver these plates into the hands of my son Omni, so they may be kept according to the commandments of my fathers.
